channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Dune: Awakening Bringing A Little Bit Of Content With Its 1.4.0 Patch Coming Mid-May
item.content:encoded:
With the major Chapter 3 update not far in the rearview mirror, the Dune: Awakening team at Funcom has shared the content that’ll arrive with an upcoming 1.4.0 patch, which arrives May 19.  

It’s important to note that this won’t be a full-on “Chapter” update. Instead, they say that “the team is focusing on highly requested features and systems, with major narrative updates planned for the future.” That said, given there won’t seem to be much to practically get our hands on, we only get a small glimpse of what’s to come so far. 

What we do get in regard to content are two new overland areas to explore. The first is The Wind Pass, a former Harkonnen tech hub, “now a key point in Arrakis’ water trade under the control of the Water Shippers.” There’s also a new scalable Testing Station, The Old Quarry, a hidden laboratory where players take on a rogue, “morally questionable” scientist named Dr. Jalanta. 

These missions do appear to come with new Landsraad missions as well; this formerly-infamous Dune: Awakening system recently got revamped with the Chapter 3 update to be more PvE-friendly. 

If you got the Season Pass, too, there’s also slick new cosmetic styles to pick up based on the Water Shippers, including a new slick ornithopter skin that’s shown off in the update trailer. 

As the Dune: Awakening team mentioned, this won’t be a content-heavy patch. But given it’s Funcom, chances are there’ll be a lot more to say about system updates once the patch is closer to its release date. Just this week, though, they did add PvE versions of the Deep Desert endgame maps, with higher rewards for those who stick to PvP. 

Something else big we’re still waiting to hear about in Dune: Awakening is the server merges, recently tested but pushed back afterwards, made possible by the recent server swap systems.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/dune-awakening-bringing-a-little-bit-of-content-with-its-140-patch-coming-mid-may-2000137949
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Name Reservations Open for EverQuest's First 2026 TLP Server, Frostreaver
item.content:encoded:
May starts tomorrow and EverQuest will be getting its first TLP server of the year, Frostreaver. The team has already begun name reservations for the new server, and released full details on when you might qualify to reserve yours before the May 27th launch.

The community voted on rules for Frostreaver, and it will start off with Standard + Scars of Velious, with new expansions unlocking every eight weeks, no Truebox, encounter locking, no open PvP, and free trade, among other rules. If you need a refresher on exactly what the rules will be for this server you can read the results of the community poll here.

Now, about those names. If you've been here before for a TLP launch or the prep period beforehand then you may be familiar with how this works. If you want to reserve a name in advance, you can do so for 499 DBC. As long as you are reserving from a live server, the name passes all creation validity checks and rules, then your reservation will be locked in for a year after the server launches.  So if you really want a certain name, this can help prevent someone else from grabbing it, and save on demand on launch day.

In addition to the DBC cost, registration is split into four tiers, with the first having opened yesterday and eligible players having already played more than 200 days or more of played time.  Next, those with more than 100 days of played time can start reserving names on May 6th. Those with 40 days of time can start reserving names on May 13th. The final group needs 10 days of played time and they can reserve on May 20th.

Frostreaver will open May 27th at noon Pacific time.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/name-reservations-open-for-everquests-first-2026-tlp-server-frostreaver-2000137947
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pearl Abyss Has Sold CCP Games Back To The Studio's Leadership Team For $120M USD
item.content:encoded:
Almost a decade after purchasing the Icelandic game studio in 2018, Pearl Abyss has sold the studio back to its leadership team for the sake of 'financial health.'

The Korean game giant bought the EVE Online developer for a final price of $225 million USD (as chronicled by MMO blogger The Nosy Gamer), which was well short of the proposed $425 million USD the sale could have been worth had CCP Games hit certain bonus metrics. Now, the sale back to its management team results in a loss, but Pearl Abyss has reason to feel optimistic by the overall experience. 

According to Korean outlet Inven Global, while EVE Online itself has been a positive in this relationship, the studio has always reported operating losses since the acquisition. This is due to the CCP Games team seeking further avenues to grow New Eden, whether through projects like the now-canceled Project Nova, its VR ventures, or the ongoing development of EVE Vanguard and EVE Frontier. 

Inven Global states that the "persistent operating losses at CCP Games" placed a "significant burden on Pearl Abyss' financial structure." By divesting from the studio in this way, the outlet reports that analysts view it as a positive, as the company has now shed a "key source of uncertainty."

The potential sale of CCP Games from Pearl Abyss has been floating around since rumors first started to circulate last summer, and it's interesting to see CCP Games themselves gain total control of their company once again. 

In a statement to Inven Global, a Pearl Abyss spokesperson said the following:

"Both companies have consistently worked to strengthen their global competitiveness under an independent management structure. After exploring various mid-to-long-term growth strategies, we concluded that selling the company to its current management is in the best interest of both parties' futures."

Pearl Abyss has reason to celebrate as it's coming off the heels of Crimson Desert, which has sold over 5 million copies of the open-world adventure game since launching in late March. CCP Games, for their part, is gearing up for next month's EVE Fanfest, which will see capsuleers of all stripes descend on the Icelandic capital of Reykjavik to learn what all is coming for New Eden in the future. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pearl-abyss-has-sold-ccp-games-back-to-the-studios-leadership-team-for-120m-usd-2000137948
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Black Desert Online Opens Arena of Solare Season 9, With Major Improvements, Adds New Node War Territories
item.content:encoded:
Black Desert Online’s latest update begins Arena of Solare Season 9, with a series of improvements to the 3v3 mode, including reworked rewards. The team also added new Node Wars territories.

Arena of Solare operates with normalized gear, so it already starts with an effort to keep things  on equal footing, making skill the determining factor.  Season 9's updates, Pearl Abyss says,  are designed to make combat better, more enjoyable, and more accessible for all skill levels.  One big change opens the battlefield rotation to six environments. Gyfin Rhasia Temple (Upper), Kermelun Wilds, Neftak Outpost, and Olun’s Valley all return, and Darkseeker’s Retreat and Yolu’s Teeth are the two new arenas. Expects Variety in the environments as well as new tactical opportunities. 

In season 9, the ranking and point systems have been reworked for transparency and reduced friction. Base point gains and losses will scale by rank, and the top Solare tier requires a qualifying score and a top 30 ranking to enter. This change is because previously, the gain / loss rate was almost uniform on all tiers, which made it really difficult to get ahead 

The team has also reworked the Arena of Solare rewards. Now, both Victory and defeat reward boxes will offer more gold bars and Seals of Solare, with an expanded Seal exchange. Those changes are intended to offer more valuable and more practical items.

All of these changes, the team promises to keep a close look on everything, especially to look out for potential balance issues. They plan to examine all classes and keep an eye on things as the season progresses. If they need to make any significant changes for balance reasons, they're also planning to do that earlier in the season.

As far as other changes, there are two new Node War territories: Ulukita and Edania. With the addition of these new battlefields, they will reorganize the existing territory groups and rotate Tier 1 and tier 3 territories. Going forward, they're going to rotate regularly to offer a reliable variety of options. 

You can check out the full patch notes for stats and info on all of these changes before you jump in.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/black-desert-online-opens-arena-of-solare-season-9-with-major-improvements-adds-new-node-war-territories-2000137945
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
